Rewa Schools Closed for Primary Classes Amid Heatwave
Digital Desk
Rewa schools closed for Classes 1–5 due to heatwave; timings revised for senior students as temperatures cross 43°C in district.
Rewa Schools Closed for Primary Classes Amid Heatwave
Rewa schools shut for Classes 1–5 as heatwave intensifies; revised timings announced for senior students under district order
Heatwave Triggers Closure
In a significant relief measure, the district administration in Rewa has announced the closure of schools for primary classes amid a sharp rise in temperature. As per the latest order, students from Classes 1 to 5 will remain on leave from April 24 to April 30, 2026, due to prevailing heatwave conditions.
The decision comes as part of a broader response to safeguard children from extreme weather, which has intensified across parts of Madhya Pradesh.
Revised School Timings
For students in Classes 6 to 12, the administration has revised school timings instead of declaring a full closure. Schools will now operate from 7:30 am to 12:00 noon during this period.
Officials stated that the move aims to minimise exposure to peak daytime heat while ensuring academic continuity. Teachers, however, will be required to remain present in schools until 1:30 pm to complete administrative and academic responsibilities.
Order Applies to All
The directive will apply uniformly across all educational institutions in the district. This includes government and private schools, as well as institutions affiliated with boards such as Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE), Council for the Indian School Certificate Examinations (ICSE), Kendriya Vidyalayas, and Navodaya Vidyalayas.
According to officials, strict compliance has been mandated, and school managements have been instructed to follow the order without exception.
Rising Temperature Concerns
The official cited alarming temperature trends as the primary reason behind the decision. On April 19, the mercury in Rewa reportedly crossed 43 degrees Celsius. Weather forecasts indicate that similar or higher temperatures may persist in the coming days.
Authorities expressed concern over the health risks posed by prolonged exposure to heat, particularly for younger children who are more vulnerable to dehydration and heat-related illnesses.
Exams to Continue
Despite the closure for primary classes, scheduled examinations and essential academic activities will continue as per the existing timetable. Officials clarified that there will be no बदलाव in exam schedules, and necessary arrangements will be made to conduct them safely.
Sources indicated that schools have been advised to ensure proper ventilation and drinking water facilities for students attending exams.
Administration’s Directive
The order, issued by the district collector, will come into effect from April 24. The administration has emphasised that the measure is precautionary and aimed at public safety.
Officials noted that similar way may be considered in other districts if temperatures continue to rise across the region.
Public Health Impact
The decision is expected to bring relief to parents and guardians concerned about children’s safety during the ongoing heatwave. Health experts have consistently warned about the risks of heatstroke, especially among younger age groups.
This development also reflects a broader trend of local administrations taking proactive steps in response to extreme weather, a growing concern highlighted in recent India News Update reports and public interest discussions.
What Lies Ahead
Authorities will review the situation at the end of April before deciding on further extensions or additional measures. Weather conditions over the next few days will play a crucial role in determining future action.
As temperatures remain high, officials have urged residents to follow safety guidelines, stay hydrated, and avoid unnecessary outdoor exposure during peak hours. The Rewa schools closure remains a key Government Updates story in Latest News Today, reflecting rising climate-related challenges.
